English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Alison Mason Kingsbury papers
Kingsbury, Alison Mason, 1898-
Markovitz, Holly.
3663
1.5 cubic feet. 1.5 cubic feet.
[ca. 1923]-1965. Manuscripts, printed materials. Drawings, illustrations, and paintings; charcoal landscape drawings; exhibition catalogs and reviews; bound volumes of sketches, notebooks and pamphlets. Also, "Unheralded Artist: Alison Kingsbury," Princeton University undergraduate thesis by Holly Markovitz, 2000. Collection material in English <<<<<<< HEAD 5 1 ? (unprocessed) ======= 5 1 ? (unprocessed) >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab 2067313
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
RMC Staff
Date completed:
June 2016
EAD encoding:
RMC Staff, June 2016
Date modified:
RMC Staff, June 2018
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
Artist.
Alison Mason Kingsbury was an artist in Ithaca, N.Y. She came to Ithaca in 1926 as a student assistant to Ezra Winter, who designed the mural in the lobby of Willard Straight Hall, Cornell University. She married Morris Bishop, a noted Cornell writer and historian.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Drawings, illustrations, and paintings; charcoal landscape drawings; exhibition catalogs and reviews; bound volumes of sketches, notebooks and pamphlets. Also, "Unheralded Artist: Alison Kingsbury," Princeton University undergraduate thesis by Holly Markovitz, 2000.
RELATED MATERIALS
See also Alison Mason Kingsbury Gannett Clinic mural materials, #43-32-1193, Alison Jolly Papers, #8517, and Morris Bishop Papers, #14-18-641. A video of Kingsbury is availible in the Cornell Artists and Local Video Programs collection, #3867.
SERIES LIST
Series I. Correspondence
Series II. Papers and Family Documents
Series III. Artwork
Series IV. Photographs
Series V. VHS Tapes
Cite As:
Alison Mason Kingsbury papers, #3663. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Sketchbooks. Reviews. Exhibition catalogs. Paintings. Illustrations. Drawings. Artists. Women artists -- New York (State) -- Ithaca. Willard Straight Hall (Ithaca, N.Y.)
